hook.text: The sun rose today. It rose yesterday. It rose every day in recorded history. Will it rise tomorrow?

buildup.text: Induction reasons from specific observations to general conclusions. It's powerful but never certain.

discovery.text: Science runs on induction. We test hypotheses with data, but new evidence can always overturn results.

twist.text: Every swan Europeans had ever seen was white—until they discovered black swans in Australia.

climax.text: Induction gives probable conclusions, never guaranteed ones. That's its limit and its honesty.

quiz.question: Why is inductive reasoning never 100% certain?
options: New evidence can always contradict the conclusion
